waypoints.note: Embarked, Force J transports forming offshore north of Juno. Centroid in the run-in approach lane.

disputedBy.claim: B Company Queen's Own Rifles of Canada is often cited as having taken the highest single-company casualties of any Allied first wave on D-Day (Nan White sector, ~2/3 of the company down within the first minutes). The exact figure is contested across QOR, brigade, and divisional war diaries ; published estimates range from 61 to 95 for B Coy alone.

waypoints.note: H-Hour Juno (delayed 10 min): 7 Brigade on Mike sectors (Courseulles), 8 Brigade on Nan sectors (Bernières / Saint-Aubin). Position approximates Bernières-sur-Mer.
Bernières-sur-Mer cleared (first French village liberated by Canadian troops) ; 9 Brigade landing in support.
Lead armour (1st Hussars Shermans) pushing through Reviers and Bény-sur-Mer toward the high ground south of the beachhead.
Lead patrols of the Inns of Court / 1st Hussars reach Anguerny / Anisy. Canadian advance is the deepest D-Day penetration of any Allied division.
End of MVP window: 3rd Cdn ID closest approach to the Caen–Carpiquet airfield; centroid south of Bény-sur-Mer. Position is approximate divisional centroid.
